_comment: Curated list of mixin handler methods that FATALLY crash on the target MC and
cannot be repaired by remapping. Retromod surgically removes the listed handler
method(s) from the mixin during transformation, so the mixin framework never
processes them — the mod loads and the one feature that handler implemented is
inert, instead of the whole game crashing.
This is the escape hatch for failures we cannot safely auto-detect — most notably
MixinExtras @WrapOperation / @ModifyExpressionValue handlers that capture a @Local
from a vanilla method whose local-variable layout changed between MC versions. The
@Local then resolves to the wrong slot and MixinExtras emits an invalid bridge
(VerifyError: Bad local variable type) at class-load time.
Users can extend or override this via config/retromod/mixin-blocklist.json (same
format). Entries from both files are merged.
Format: each entry needs a 'mixin' (the mixin class, '/' or '.' separated). If
'methods' is given, only those handler methods are stripped (surgical, preferred).
If 'methods' is omitted/empty, every injector handler on the class is stripped.
Add '"strip": "class"' to neutralize the WHOLE mixin instead of stripping
handlers — Retromod repoints the @Mixin annotation at a non-existent target so
the framework skips it entirely (the harmless 'target not found' path). Use this
for mixins that ADD AN INTERFACE to their target (handler-stripping leaves the
interface unapplied → ClassCastException downstream, #68) or that have an
interdependent critical @Inject failure where partial stripping leaves orphans (#69).

blocked.reason: MixinExtras @WrapOperation + @Local Level on HangingEntityItem.useOn, wrapping ItemStack.shrink(I). MC 26.1 restructured useOn, so the @Local Level resolves to a non-existent slot and MixinExtras emits an invalid bridge (VerifyError: Bad local variable type) that crashes at bootstrap. Stripping just this handler lets Deeper and Darker boot; the painting stack-decrement tweak is inert.

@Inject handlers on Entity.addAdditionalSaveData/readAdditionalSaveData capture CompoundTag, but MC moved those params to ValueOutput/ValueInput in the 1.21.5 serialization (ValueIO/codec) refactor -> InvalidInjectionException (Invalid descriptor: expected ValueOutput, found CompoundTag) on 1.21.11. Not a rename (ValueOutput has a different API than CompoundTag), so the handler body can't be auto-retyped yet. Stripping these handlers removes the injection error. NOTE (#48): Darker Depths' death-anchor feature is interdependent (the stripped handlers leave orphaned helper lambdas + a @Unique field/interface), so this mod still does not fully construct after the strip — it's a PARTIAL soft-fail, kept here because the handlers are genuinely broken and the strip is correct as far as it goes. Mods with a self-contained broken handler soft-fail cleanly.

@ModifyReturnValue handler on a getDefaultDimensions/getDimensions method (returns EntityDimensions) fails injection on 1.21.11 with 'failed injection check, (0/1) succeeded. Scanned 0 target(s)' — the target method couldn't be resolved on the newer MC (renamed/re-signatured; mod ships no refMap). The handler body only rescales the dimensions' EntityAttachments via the mod's own Accessors util — it does NOT touch the mod's shared-goals feature (revamped_phantoms$goalHolder/registerGoals/getGoalHolder/HasSharedGoals), which injects fine — so this is a CLEAN, self-contained soft-fail: stripping it lets Revamped Phantoms construct with goals intact and only the phantom-size tweak inert. Contrast #48, which was interdependent.

@Inject handler revamped_phantoms$onHurtsTarget fails injection on 1.21.11 as a CRITICAL failure: 'InjectionError: failed injection check, (0/1) succeeded. Scanned 0 target(s). No refMap loaded' — the target method signature changed in vanilla and the mod ships no refMap, so it can't be repaired by remapping. Confirmed against the #69 re-open log (the crash that took down DataComponents/EntityType <clinit>). Upgraded from handler-strip to whole-class strip ('strip':'class'): the sweep-attack tweak is a single self-contained mixin, so neutralizing the whole class is equivalent in scope but guarantees the critical injection can never fire regardless of any @Unique/helper interdependency. Revamped Phantoms loads with the sweep-attack tweak inert; PhantomMixin's shared-goals + size features stay live via the separate handler-strip entry above.

True Darkness (grondag.darkness, the base mod that True Darkness Biomes depends on). MixinLightTexture does '@Mixin(LightTexture.class) implements LightmapAccess' — it makes the vanilla LightTexture implement darkness's LightmapAccess duck-interface. On 1.21.11 it fails to apply ('InvalidMixinException: @Shadow field lightTexture was not located in the target class LightTexture. No refMap loaded') because the shadowed field's surrounding structure changed and the mod ships no refMap. A field rename can't fix it (the structure moved, not just the name), and handler-stripping is useless here — there are no injector handlers, the mixin's whole job is adding the interface. Whole-class strip neutralizes it cleanly. Pairs with the MixinGameRenderer entry below.

True Darkness's MixinGameRenderer.onRenderLevel handler DID apply, and on first level render it casts the vanilla LightTexture to grondag.darkness.LightmapAccess -> ClassCastException (the actual fatal crash in #68, on world load). The cast only fails because MixinLightTexture (above) couldn't add the interface. Even if handler-stripping removed onRenderLevel, darkness's lighting would be half-wired; neutralizing the whole render-mixin pair is the clean soft-fail: True Darkness's darker-lighting feature goes inert but the mod loads and the game runs (vs the current crash-on-world-create). MixinDynamicTexture / MixinEndEffects / MixinNetherEffects are left alone — Effects already skip gracefully (their target inner classes moved), DynamicTexture applies fine.
